input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule V.4.1 specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.4.1
            TITLE: wheel size

            CONTENT:
            rule V.4.1: Wheel Size
V.4.1 Wheel Size 
Wheels must be 203.2 mm (8.0 inches) or more in diameter.

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, wheel outline

final_answer:
Explanation:Rule V.4.1 mandates that the wheels must have a minimum diameter of 203.2 mm. Using the overall length of the vehicle (3202.4 mm) as a scale reference, the diameter of the wheels depicted in the engineering drawing is estimated to be approximately 200 mm. Since 200 mm is less than the required minimum diameter of 203.2 mm, the design does not comply with Rule V.4.1.
Answer:No
